---
title: "Dynamic Year"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
base-year: 2026
shortcodes:
  - dynamic-year.lua
---

## Quarto Shortcodes

This document sets `base-year: 2026` in its YAML front matter. The examples
below show the shortcode source first, followed by the rendered value.
The source blocks include a space between the opening braces so Quarto displays
them instead of executing them.

### Years

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-year 0 >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-year 0 >}}

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-year -1 >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-year -1 >}}

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-year 1 >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-year 1 >}}

### Dates

The `dynamic-date` shortcode replaces the `YYYY` placeholder with the computed
year while keeping the month and day.

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-date "YYYY-10-15" >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-date "YYYY-10-15" >}}

Offsets work for dates as well.

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-date "YYYY-10-15" -1 >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-date "YYYY-10-15" -1 >}}

Invalid dates fail by default. You can choose how to repair them when rendering
dates such as February 30.

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-date "YYYY-02-30" invalid="previous-month-end" >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-date "YYYY-02-30" invalid="previous-month-end" >}}

```markdown
{ {< dynamic-date "YYYY-02-30" invalid="next-month-start" >}}
```

Result: {{< dynamic-date "YYYY-02-30" invalid="next-month-start" >}}

## R Helpers

```{r}
source("dynamic-year.R")

dynamic_year()
dynamic_year(-1)
dynamic_date("YYYY")
dynamic_date("YYYY-10-15")
dynamic_date(as.Date("2019-10-15"))
dynamic_date("YYYY-02-30", invalid = "previous-month-end")
dynamic_date("YYYY-02-30", invalid = "next-month-start")
```
